_codex_notes: Removed inferred location/country/ask/risk. Fixed team to include all 4 members. Removed unsupported industryPriorityAreas. Traction cleaned to separate current state from future plans.
oneLiner: Applied Intelligence helps small and midsize manufacturers turn untapped operational data into measurable operational gains. We deliver tailored AI software solutions across areas like quality control, predictive maintenance, and supply chain optimization, focused on data-rich firms that lack dedicated data science resources.
customers: Small and midsize manufacturers (SMEs) in data-rich sectors that lack dedicated data science resources. Starting with ~1,300 manufacturing firms in North Dakota, then scaling nationally to 9M+ US small businesses in data-rich sectors.
pricing: Needs Assessments: $3,000-$20,000. Application & Implementation: custom AI system deployments. Research & Advancement contracts: ongoing upskilling and R&D collaboration (recurring revenue).
traction: First enterprise lead (Agri-Cover) in pipeline for paid pilot. Planning $500K pre-seed round in early 2026. Targeting 2-3 additional paying customers.
team: Ayman Sajjad Akash, CEO: PhD candidate in Data Science, 2.5 years in technical sales and manufacturing. Dr. Harun Pirim, Chief Science Officer: 15+ years in AI and network science. Yusuf Akbulut, CTO: Software engineer, 5+ years translating AI models into scalable solutions. M. Mishkatur Rahman, COO: 3 years in manufacturing and supply chain.
advantage: Fills the 'missing middle' between expensive enterprise AI platforms (C3.ai, Siemens) and rigid point solutions (Augury, MachineMetrics). Uses generative AI agents to build custom solutions faster than boutique dev shops. PhD-level predictive modeling with white-box transparency.
competitors: C3.ai, Siemens, Augury, MachineMetrics, Applied Engineering
keywords: Artificial Intelligence, B2B, Big Data, Enterprise Software, Industrial Automation, Machine Learning, Manufacturing, Predictive Analytics, SaaS
extraContext: TAM: $90B (9M+ US small businesses in data-rich sectors). SAM: $13M+ (1,300 manufacturing firms in North Dakota). $500K pre-seed round planned for early 2026.

_codex_notes: Fixed funding to Pre-seed (<$500K) per $8K bootstrapping closed. Removed unsupported location/country/yearFounded. BusinessModel changed to reflect direct sales. Revenue left empty (unsupported). Fundraising status clarified.
oneLiner: Bossin Hooks creates sensory toys for therapists and parents that are calm, customizable, and clinically effective. Handmade crochet sensory tools help individuals stay calm and engaged at home and in therapy sessions. Unlike mass-market toys that break, overstimulate, or distract, Bossin Hooks products are quiet, durable, and tailored to individual sensory needs.
customers: Two segments: (1) Therapists and clinics (713K licensed US professionals) treating ADHD, Autism, SPD, Trauma. (2) Parents and families (10M US families) managing sensory needs at home. Future: schools, daycares, hospitals ($500M+ opportunity).
pricing: Individual tools: $8-$25. Starter kits: $130. Quarterly Subscription Kits (beta 2026): $250. Gross profit margins averaging 50%.
traction: 650+ tools sold to parents. 380+ tools sold to therapy clinics. 6 clinic clients. 175+ interviews with therapists and parents. $8K bootstrapped (closed). Projecting $750K revenue run rate by end of 2026.
team: Jessica Christoffersen, Founder & CEO: Former paraeducator, crochet artisan, neurodivergent founder. Built initial traction in six clinics, conducted 175+ interviews, leads design and operations.
advantage: Handmade craftsmanship with consistent quality and professional presentation. Fully customizable by color, texture, and firmness for individual sensory needs. Direct therapist/parent feedback loop. National safety standards compliance.
competitors: Fat Brain Toys (Target/Amazon mass market), Lakeshore Learning, Fun and Function (therapy catalogs), Etsy independent makers
keywords: B2B, B2C, Child Care, Consumer Goods, Handmade, Health Care, Subscription Service, Wellness
extraContext: Comparables: Melissa & Doug acquired by Spin Master for $950M (Oct 2023). Lakeshore Learning acquired by Leonard Green & Partners ($680M). Rubik's Brand acquired by Spin Master for $50M (Jan 2021). $500K pre-seed planned (future). $2M seed planned Q2 2027.

_codex_notes: Removed inferred location/country/yearFounded/ask/risk. All verifiable from source doc.
oneLiner: Premier Technologies delivers next-generation liquid fertilizer flow monitoring systems that empower farmers to apply nutrients with 99.7% accuracy. The platform provides real-time, row-by-row visibility streamed wirelessly to an iPad, ensuring every drop of fertilizer is applied precisely, maximizing yields and minimizing waste.
customers: Farmers/growers planting 2,000+ acres of corn and beans. Dealer network across North America, with interest from Brazil and Australia.
pricing: Average sale price: $8,490 per system. Sold through established dealer network. Future revenue from software subscription services.
traction: Self-funded MVP developed and field-tested ($150K pre-seed closed Q1 2025). Dealer interest accelerating with growing pre-sale list. Launch inquiries increasing weekly. International interest from Brazil and Australia.
team: Kelly Sipman, CEO: 30 years business experience, previously owned Intelligent Ag (ag technology company). Travis Moszer, CSO: 20 years agriculture industry, 10 years ag tech. Combined: 50+ years experience, $200M+ in product sales.
advantage: 99.7% application accuracy vs competitors' 30% variance. Compatible with most planters (old and new), not locked to single manufacturer. Easiest flow monitor to install in the industry. Pays for itself within a single growing cycle.
competitors: Wilger Red Ball System, John Deere Exact Rate
keywords: AgTech, Agriculture, Farm Machinery, Farm Operations, Hardware, IoT/Remote Sensing, SaaS, Sensor
extraContext: TAM: $1.47B. SAM: $589M (40% penetration). SOM: $71M (12% of SAM, 10-year). Comparables: Headsight Harvest acquired by Precision Planting for $16M (2021). Appareo Solutions acquired by AGCO for $61.4M (2022). Seeking $1M seed round.

_codex_notes: Removed inferred location/country/yearFounded/industryPriorityAreas/ask/risk. Fixed team to include all members and advisor.
oneLiner: CP Ag Tech automates greenhouse phenotyping with modular robots that help crop researchers replace manual scouting with real-time image-based data. The flagship platform Dronwer 2.0 reduces up to 60% of labor costs and accelerates research decisions by delivering environmental and crop-level insights through a user-friendly dashboard.
customers: University crop researchers and breeding programs (50-60 US universities with greenhouse breeding programs). Secondary: public research centers, commercial breeding programs, and commercial greenhouses/CEA operations.
pricing: Dronwer 2.0 units: $5,000 each. Annual software & data dashboard subscription: $1,600/unit. Optional service & maintenance: $1,000/year. Total ~$8K per professor annually.
traction: Validated 3 prototypes across 3 NDSU research sites. Proven adaptability in greenhouses and field trials with canola, flax, and dry beans. $210K pre-seed round open.
team: Cristhian Perdigon, Co-Founder & CEO: M.S. in Agricultural & Biosystems Engineering (NDSU), led drone-based phenotyping research, previously founded Apocampo (Colombia). Jithin Mathew, Co-Founder & CTO: PhD candidate in Ag & Biosystems Engineering (NDSU), expertise in ML, computer vision, mechanical design. Autumn Perdigon, Co-Founder & Marketing Director: B.A. in English, leads marketing and brand strategy. Advisor: Paulo Flores, Associate Professor at NDSU, 15+ years phenotyping experience.
advantage: Affordable modular solution ($5-10K) vs competitors at $100K+. Ground-based continuous operation (no flight/battery constraints like drones). Modular design adaptable to different greenhouse layouts. Bridge from research to commercial-scale agriculture.
competitors: Corvus Drones (greenhouse drones), LemnaTec (high-end phenotyping), Phenospex (large-scale phenotyping), manual scouting (status quo)
keywords: AgTech, Agriculture, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision, Drones, Hardware, Machine Learning, Robotics, Robotics & Automation, Sensor, SaaS
extraContext: TAM: $30B global CEA + $20B precision ag by 2030. SAM: $5-10M US. SOM: $0.5-0.75M. Comparables: Sentera acquired by John Deere (2025). Aurea Imaging acquired by Hiphen (2025). LemnaTec acquired by Nynomic AG (2019). Envonics acquired by RussKap Water (2023).

_codex_notes: Fixed businessModel to Services (equipment + material supply, no software). Fixed currentlyFundraising to No (pre-seed marked Future). Removed inferred location/country/ask/risk.
oneLiner: EcoGrow reduces water pollution by converting high-phosphate wastewater into slow-release fertilizer. Using a proprietary calcium peroxide nanoparticle material, we remove 99.7% of phosphates in under five minutes at $6-8 per pound, up to 5x cheaper than conventional methods. Zero energy, no chemicals, no special training required.
customers: 1,200 dairy/cheese facilities in WI/MN/ND facing phosphate regulations (paying $30K-50K/year in penalties). Broader: 9,000 CAFOs, 2,000 food processors, 1,500 municipal wastewater treatment plants.
pricing: Equipment sales/leasing: $5K-15K one-time. Monthly material supply: $2K-8K/month recurring. Quarterly service: $500/month. Treatment cost: $6-8/lb phosphate removed (vs $12-60/lb competitors).
traction: 99.7% phosphate removal achieved in lab at treatment dose of 0.75 g/L across pH 4-8. Preparing for field pilots Q1 2026. ND Water Resources Research Institute Fellow with access to 50+ utilities. Winner of 2025 3MT Grand Championship and 2025 Possibility Fellowship.
team: Aditya Goyal, Founder & CEO: PhD candidate in Materials & Nanotechnology at NDSU. Fellow at ND Water Resources Research Institute with access to 50+ utilities across Upper Midwest. Winner of 2025 3MT Grand Championship and 2025 Possibility Fellowship.
advantage: 99.7% phosphate removal in 5 minutes. $6-8/lb cost regardless of concentration (competitors scale linearly from $12-60/lb). Zero energy, no chemicals, no training needed. Converts waste into reusable slow-release fertilizer (revenue from byproduct). Proprietary calcium peroxide nanoparticle material produced at $1.50/kg.
competitors: Kemira, Veolia, SUEZ (chemical precipitation), EBPR (biological removal), Phoslock (adsorbents), BlueGreen/SePRO (algaecides)
keywords: Agriculture, CleanTech, Clean Energy, Environmental Consulting, Environmental Engineering, GreenTech, Nanotechnology, Recycling, Sustainability, Waste Management, Water, Water Purification
extraContext: TAM: $1.9B. SAM: $42M (Upper Midwest). Comparables: Ostara acquired by Wheatsheaf Group for $30M (2022, $10M revenue). Cambrian Innovation acquired by XPV Water Partners (2021, ~$15M revenue). BlueInGreen acquired by Chart Industries for $20M (2020, $8M revenue). Conservative $20-30M exit at $8-10M revenue = 20-30x returns for pre-seed at $2-3M cap.

_codex_notes: Removed inferred country/yearFounded/ask/risk. Fixed industryPriorityAreas (not stated in source). Fixed team to include both co-founders. Industry kept as Digital Health (closest match for AI surgical + biomaterials).
oneLiner: Elevate BioTech improves orthopedic ligament repair through AI-assisted surgical planning and regenerative biomaterials. The platform provides AI-driven surgical planning tools and advanced biomimetic scaffolds for ACL reconstruction, supporting 250,000+ US ACL surgeries per year.
customers: High-volume hospitals and orthopedic clinics performing ACL reconstruction. Starting with 200 early-adopter clinics in the Midwest performing 2-3 procedures per month. Target users: orthopedic surgeons and sports medicine teams.
pricing: AI surgical software: $1,200 per annual license to hospitals/clinics. COGS: $180. Gross margins: 85%.
traction: Software finalization in progress. Filing patent. Class II 510(k) regulatory pathway initiated. Initial preclinical validations underway. $1M pre-seed round open (2025 Q4).
team: Pooyan Vahidi Pashaki, Founder & CEO: Biomedical engineer and material scientist with expertise in biomaterials, advanced manufacturing, and tissue engineering. Led multiple research projects on biomimetic scaffolds. Benjamin Vahidi Pashaki, Co-Founder & Chief AI Officer: AI and data science expert, experience in ML algorithms, decision-support systems, and predictive modeling. Designed scalable AI platforms for clinical workflows.
advantage: End-to-end continuum (pre, intra, post-op) unlike competitors. AI-driven surgical planning with graft size and tunnel diameter optimization. Compatible with any biomimetic scaffold. Patient-specific biomechanical and imaging data integration. Class II 510(k) pathway avoids costly Class III process.
competitors: Stryker Ortho Q / Ortho Guidance Software, Arthrex Surgeon App & Planning Tools
keywords: Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Health Care, Health Diagnostics, Medical, Medical Device, SaaS, Software
extraContext: TAM: $10B global ACL regeneration + AI surgical optimization (6% CAGR). SAM: $4B US (500K ACL repairs/year). SOM: $40M (200 early-adopter clinics). Comparables: Biorez acquired by Smith+Nephew for $140M (2022, ~$5M revenue). $1M pre-seed open, $5M seed planned 2027, $10M Series A planned 2028.
